query: evidence on cobalt blue light slit-lamp biomicroscopy for mesopic pupil size measurement

retrieved_docs.title: Tonometry -- Equipment -- Applanation Tonometry
retrieved_docs.content: Eye clinicians use GAT as a standard component of a slit-lamp (See slit-lamp and GAT tonometer images) eye exam. [7] To perform GAT, topical anesthetic drops and a fluorescein dye are first applied to the eyes. Filtered cobalt blue light is then used to visualize the fluorescein dye while the tonometer tip compresses the center of the cornea with a diameter of 3.06 mm (See GAT tip images). The blue light and dye highlight the circular border of the tear film created by the applanator tip pressed against the cornea (See GAT tip images). A prism in the tip splits this circular film of fluorescein into two green semicircles when visualized from the slit lamp oculars (See images with arcs). The clinician then alters the tonometer force against the cornea by turning the dial on the side of the instrument (See tonometer with dial images) until the two semicircles overlap slightly (See images with arcs). This slight overlap of the two semicircles indicates that the cornea has been flattened by a calibrated amount to give an accurate IOP measurement in mmHg, which is read on the dial (see tonometer with dial images) of the tonometer. [29]

retrieved_docs.title: Slit-Lamp Biomicroscope -- Equipment -- Filters
retrieved_docs.content: The illumination beam is provided with different kinds of filters. The cobalt blue and red-free filters are provided in most models.

retrieved_docs.title: Myopia -- Evaluation -- Slit-lamp Examination
retrieved_docs.content: The iridocorneal angle can be evaluated using the oblique slit in a preferably dark environment using corneal thickness as a reference. Gross evaluation of the central anterior chamber depth can be performed; however, Pentacam or optical biometers are necessary for quantification and screening. Lenticular assessment is the most critical component of slit-lamp examination. Progressive myopia in the older population may be caused by the cataractous transformation of the crystalline lens. Nuclear expansion of the crystalline lens helps unaided near vision in older patients, also termed “second sight.” Slit-lamp biomicroscopic evaluation of the posterior pole and optic disc can be performed using a 90-D fundoscopic lens, which is the most widely used lens in ophthalmic practice for easier evaluation of the posterior pole in undilated pupils. [38]

retrieved_docs.title: Hyperopia -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: 2. Surgical treatment: Preoperative stable refraction (cycloplegic and manifest) [43] is must at least in three successive checkups one year apart. Counseling and discussion about possible outcomes and side effects are very important. Before any surgical intervention, every patient should undergo Slit-lamp biomicroscopy: to rule out allergic blepharoconjunctivitis and dry eye syndromes [44] Refraction testing (cycloplegic and manual) both distant and near vision Corneal thickness and topography, wavefront analysis [45] Intraocular pressure Pupil size estimation in dark and mesopic conditions Fundoscopy So, all the investigations are of utmost importance before any sort of kerato-refractive surgery.

retrieved_docs.title: Marcus Gunn Pupil -- Evaluation -- Limitations
retrieved_docs.content: Modern pupillography uses a computerized device to simulate the swinging flashlight test and allows more accurate detection and quantification of RAPDs. [41] These are binocular pupillometers which provide information on pupil diameter, pupil diameter vs. time curves, asymmetry in pupillary responses. The data studied in pupillography includes average pupillary constriction amplitude (CA), constriction velocity (CV), and constriction onset latency (COL). CA is the best parameter for detecting RAPDs and differentiating patients from normal subjects. [42]

retrieved_docs.title: Tests for Potential Vision -- Procedures -- Procedure
retrieved_docs.content: Blue field entopic phenomenon – The patient is asked to look at a bright and diffusely illuminated surface with accommodation relaxed. The background is illuminated by blue light (350 to 450 nm), which is maximally absorbed by hemoglobin.

retrieved_docs.title: The Effect of Pupil Size on Visual Resolution -- Issues of Concern -- Clinical Examination and Testing
retrieved_docs.content: Pupil size can be evaluated clinically using various methods. The handheld light examination provides a gross estimate of pupil size and reactivity to light. Quantifying pupil size in light and dark conditions is integral to the comprehensive pupillary examination. Pupil diameter is measured in millimeters using a pupil gauge typically printed on a near vision reading card or dedicated tool. [8] This technique provides a consistent and reproducible method for measuring pupil size, enabling monitoring of changes in pupillary response over time.

retrieved_docs.title: The Effect of Pupil Size on Visual Resolution -- Issues of Concern -- Retinal Luminance
retrieved_docs.content: Pupil size influences the magnitude of electrical responses in full-field electroretinogram (ffERG) testing, which measures cone cell function in the retina. A study involving the Diopsys ® NOVA fixed-luminance flicker ffERG device found that the measured electrical response increased with pupil size in terms of pupil diameter and pupil area. This suggests the need to consider pupil size when conducting and interpreting ffERG testing, with specific reference ranges potentially enhancing the clinical utility of such tests. [46]

retrieved_docs.title: Epiphora Clinical Testing -- Procedures -- Fluorescein Dye Disappearance Test
retrieved_docs.content: After a drop of 2% fluorescein has been placed in the fornix, the fluorescein remaining in the conjunctival cul-de-sac is examined with the cobalt blue light after five minutes. The amount of remaining fluorescein can be graded using a scale from 0 to 4, where 0 indicates no remaining dye, and 4 indicates all the dye remains. This is also compared to the opposite eye. Retention of the fluorescein indicates a delay in tear flow. If the result is normal, lacrimal drainage dysfunction is unlikely. [4] The Jones I & II Tests These tests assess the flow of tears into the lacrimal sac and through the nasolacrimal duct. The Jones I Test

retrieved_docs.title: Auxiliary Lenses for Slit-Lamp Examination of the Retina  -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: 90D lens : It is a widely used and the most popular slit lamp fundus examination lens. Its image magnification is 0.76x. Though the lens actually minifies the retinal image, a magnified image of the retina may be visualized using the magnification system of the slit lamp. The magnification of the fundus image is markedly influenced by axial ametropia and the distance of the lens from the examined eye. Therefore, a correction factor ( p ) is used to estimate the actual optic disc size. [10] Its small diameter ring is ideal for dynamic examination and easy manipulation within the orbit. It can be used on small pupils.

retrieved_docs.content: In a later experiment, participants viewed an image of an expanding black hole, which imparts the impression of a progressively deepening darkness, even though the image is static. Similarly, the participants' pupils dilated in response to the perceived darkness rather than the actual luminance of the image, which remained constant. [117] Additional studies have expanded on observations into perception and pupil size. For example, pupil constriction is seen when viewing a picture of the sun. [119] Generating mental images of light and dark scenarios affects pupil size. [120]

retrieved_docs.content: However, these methods cannot be applied in a routine clinical setting. The slit lamp lenses can easily determine the size of the optic disc by overcoming the high focal convergence of the cornea. However, these magnify the image by a certain factor. While evaluating the optic disc, the disc and cup size has to be noted while calculating the cup disc (C:D) ratio. The lens's magnification factor must be considered while estimating the true optic disc size. [19]

retrieved_docs.title: Corneal Injury -- Evaluation -- Corneal Abrasion and Laceration/Perforation
retrieved_docs.content: Conjunctival erythema, lid swelling, tearing, and blepharospasm can be seen upon eye inspection. The corneal defect can often be visualised under magnification even without the use of fluorescein. With severe pain, treatment with a topical anesthetic may be needed to obtain a complete physical exam. Relief of pain with the application of a topical anaesthetic such as proparacaine strongly suggests a superficial (corneal) etiology of ocular pain. Visual acuity should be considered the vital sign of the eye and be determined in every patient with an ocular complaint. Acuity should be assessed while the patient is wearing any corrective lenses should they have them and is typically normal in the setting of corneal abrasions unless there is associated iritis or corneal defect in the central visual axis. Instillation of fluorescein and examination with a cobalt blue light will demonstrate the corneal abrasion as a bright green, superficial defect of the cornea. [17] [18] [19] [20]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 2 -- Community 954
retrieved_docs.content: Ibopamine and cyclopentolate are both classified as mydriatics, meaning they are pharmacologic agents used to dilate the pupil. As mydriatic drugs, they act on the iris muscles to produce pupil enlargement, which is valuable in ophthalmic examinations and certain therapeutic procedures. Their shared classification underscores a common mechanism of action and similar clinical applications in eye care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1628
retrieved_docs.content: Cykloplegika are used as mydriatics and cycloplegics, serving to dilate the pupil and temporarily paralyze accommodation during ophthalmic examinations and procedures.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 3 -- Community 1262
retrieved_docs.content: Cyklopentolate is classified as a mydriatic, indicating that it is used to induce dilation of the pupil.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 2710
retrieved_docs.content: Atropine and cyclopentolate are both classified as mydriatics, a group of agents that produce pupil dilation. As mydriatic drugs, they are used in ophthalmic practice to achieve mydriasis for diagnostic examinations, therapeutic procedures, and the management of certain eye conditions, reflecting their shared pharmacologic role in facilitating ocular assessment and treatment.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1579

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1960
retrieved_docs.content: Fysostigmin and isofluorofát are classified as members of the pharmacological group “Parasympathomimetics, antiglaucoma preparations and miotics.” This category comprises agents that mimic parasympathetic activity, are employed in the treatment of glaucoma, and produce miosis (pupil constriction). By acting as cholinergic agonists or acetylcholinesterase inhibitors, these drugs increase aqueous humor outflow and lower intra‑ocular pressure, thereby helping to manage glaucomatous optic neuropathy. Recognizing fysostigmin and isofluorofát as part of this class guides clinicians in selecting appropriate therapies for patients requiring parasympathomimetic or miotic effects, particularly in ocular conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 1296
retrieved_docs.content: Therapeutic role is a broad category of medical action that encompasses specific functions such as the Thrombolytic therapeutic role, which is a distinct type of therapeutic activity aimed at dissolving blood clots. The agent cykloplegika also fulfills a therapeutic role; it is employed in clinical practice as a mydriatic and cycloplegic agent, meaning it is used to dilate the pupil and temporarily paralyze the eye’s accommodation mechanism. Thus, cykloplegika’s use as mydriatics and cycloplegics exemplifies how a therapeutic role can be manifested through specific pharmacologic applications.
